title: Fluorescent staining for Callose with aniline blue
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6uqk4","text":"Fluorescent staining for Callose of sieve tubes of phloem.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":58}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c1bll","text":"Calose (β-1→3-glucan) is the distinguished polysaccharide present on the sieve plates, of each sieve tube member of phloem tissue.  Aniline blue stain specifically callose and it is use as a marker for identification of sieve elements of phloem.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"882ti","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d15oj","text":"PROCEDURE – STAINING FOR CALLOSE WITH ANILINE BLUE (modification of Klaus Herburger and Andreas Holzinger (2016).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":113}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2ouij","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2skjt","text":"Prepare Sørensen’s phosphate buffer (0.1 M, pH=8.0) according with Ruzin (1999).  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f1fep","text":"Stock solutions. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d78kp","text":"A) 0.2 M of NaH2PO4 (Sodium phosphate monobasic anhydrous, CAS Number: 7558-80-7) BioUltra, ≥99.0% (T) (Sigma-Aldrich S3139).","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"86vch","text":"B) 0.2 M of Na2HPO4 (Sodium phosphate dibasic anhydrous, CAS Number: 7558-79-4) Ultra ≥99.5%, (Sigma-Aldrich S7907).","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"781g2","text":"To prepare 100 mL of the buffer (0.1 M, pH=8.0), mix 2.7 mL of stock solution (A) with 47.3 mL of stock solution (B) and adjust volume to 100 mL  in a Schott flask.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":164}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1mri8","text":"Add 0.5 % Aniline blue (w/v) to freshly prepared Sørensen’s phosphate buffer.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4mh0q","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fis8v","text":"Fix tissue in 2.5% glutaraldehyde in phosphate buffer Sorensen's solution (0.1 M, pH 7.2) and processed for LR white resin embedding according with manufactures.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"anqva","text":"Obtain transverse semi thin sections (1 µm) with an ultramicrotome and bond the sections to microscope grass slides with heat (60 C) during 15 min.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9hlnv","text":"Stain sections with 0.5 % of Aniline blue (CAS Number: 28631-66-5, Sigma Aldrich, Germany) in Sørensen’s phosphate buffer (0.1 M, pH=8.0) for 1 h under low light intensities (~5 μmol photons m-2 s-1) to prevent degradation of the dye.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":192,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":196,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dtkl5","text":"Observe tissue under an Epifluorescence Microscope (Axioscope 2 Plus, Carl Zeiss AG) using a Set 01 (excitation: band pass (BP) 365/12 nm; emission: long pass (LP) 397 nm) and connected to an Axiocam MRc5 camera. Calose of sieve elements stain bright green yellow. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"90iqc","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4v77","text":"References","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bg96r","text":"Herburger, K., \u0026 Holzinger, A. (2016). Aniline blue and Calcofluor white staining of callose and cellulose in the streptophyte green algae Zygnema and Klebsormidium. Bio-Protocol, 6(20), e1969. http://doi.org/10.21769/BioProtoc.1969","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
creator: Hilda Araceli AZM Zavaleta Mancera
